title: Confidentiality
content:
The specifications, definitions and examples in this manual are to be used by the recipient for the sole 
purpose of programming to meet the requirements of DHL, and should not be altered or modified by the 
recipient.
This document contains confidential, proprietary information. No part of this document may be used, 
modified, reproduced, disseminated, or disclosed without the express written consent of DHL. DHL 
employees, third-party vendors, and others authorized to use this document shall only disclose this 
information internally to their coworkers, employees, or agents on a need-to-know basis and in keeping 
with DHL confidentiality requirements. The information in this document is subject to change without 
notice.
DHL SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR TECHNICAL OR EDITORIAL ERRORS OR OMISSIONS 
CONTAINED HEREIN NOR FOR INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES RESULTING FROM 
THE USE OF THIS INFORMATION.

title: Audience
content:
This document is intended to serve as a guide to assist DHL customers and third party developers during 
the integration development process. This document contains the record layout of DHL Global Standard 
Manifesting messages. The primary audience of this document is intended to be a trading partner’s 
technical resources and internal DHL EDI support staff.

title: Introduction
content:
This specification provides the definition of the global standard DHL manifesting EDI messages. This is a 
guide to be used in satisfying the requirements when exchanging electronic data to become a certified 
trading partner with DHL and its partners. The data contained in these EDI messages consist of 
manifesting information related to the transportation of cargo and transport related charges.
For purposes of this document, “Charges”  means all applicable transportation and other charges, 
including but not limited to, all applicable accessorial charges, surcharges, additional handling charges, 
late payment fees, and collection costs including collection agency fees, legal fees, court costs, credit 
reports, and bad check collection fees, in accordance with applicable laws.
Notwithstanding any billing plan that is in effect or payment or billing option selected at the time of 
shipment, the shipper is ultimately liable for and agrees to pay all Charges, including in the event of 
insolvency, bankruptcy, or non-payment or refusal to pay by the consignee or third party.
All Charges must be ‘paid in full’ (per the invoice), in the lawful currency noted as the billing currency.
Global EDI IFCSUM D01B v3.0             Confidential                             Page 11 of 115
Message Specification Guidelines

title: Definition of EDI
content:
EDI is the computer to computer exchange of business transactions between organizations in an agreed 
upon format. "Computer-to-computer" means that the data sent or received is communicated via 
electronic transmission, without human intervention or interpretation.
Maintaining standardized formats is the foundation of a Global EDI service. EDI requires business 
partners to follow the standards as defined by the format layout and content held within. After integrating 
onto the EDI platform, data will be converted by EDI translation programs into the exact same format and 
provided by each country. Industry standard formats have been defined by EDIFACT, ANSI and 
RosettaNet.

title: Service Terms and Conditions
content:
Unless otherwise agreed in writing, the transportation products and services mentioned in this document 
are subject to DHL’s Terms and Conditions of Carriage. Since these may vary depending on location of 
the country of origin of the shipment, please contact the nearest DHL service center to obtain a copy of 
the local terms and conditions. Not all of DHL’s products and services may be available in every country.
It is the customer’s responsibility to complete an appropriate amount of testing and inform DHL when their 
systems are ready to send and/or receive the electronic files. DHL will provide test files as they become 
available until customer has completed their testing and provides written confirmation that they are ready 
to be moved to production status.

title: IFCSUM Description
content:
This document is part of the document “EDI Implementation Guide for customer interfaces”. It provides 
the definition of the International Forwarding and Consolidation Summary Message (IFCSUM) to be used 
in Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) between Deutsche Post World Net partners and its customers. 
 
Therefore, this specification can be used for EDI exchange between customers and Deutsche Post 
subsidiaries. The Deutsche Post World Net partner takes care of the EDI exchange to DHL Express and 
other partners in this group. 
 
Within this document the IFCSUM declaration of data elements will be described along with branching 
diagrams to show the arrangement of segments and segment groups. It also contains detailed 
descriptions of segment and assignment information for individual data elements.
Global EDI IFCSUM D01B v3.0             Confidential                             Page 14 of 115
Message Specification Guidelines

title: Mandatory/Optional Fields
content:
M Mandatory - This item must be present in all data transactions - used for critical  
data which is also Mandatory in the EDIFACT standard. 
 
R Required - This item must be present in all data transactions. In the opinion of the implementation 
guide designers it is essential for this business use of the transaction set, however not defined as 
Mandatory in the EDIFACT standard.  
 
C Conditional - This item is the subject of a syntax rule (a relational condition) which links its usage with 
that of other items. If not used a data element separator giving its position must still be given, unless it 
appears at the end of a segment in which case the data element separator is also not required. 
 
X Not Used - This item is not used in a data transaction. A data element separator giving its position must 
still be given, but no content should be given, unless it appears at the end of a segment in which case the 
data element separator is also not required.

title: Units of Measure
content:
The following codes are valid for unit of measurement for weight, volume and dimensions
Weight
Default = KGM (Kilograms) 
Also acceptable: LBR (Pounds)
Volume
Default = MTQ (Cubic Meters) 
Also acceptable: CMQ (Cubic Centimeters), INQ (Cubic Inches)
Dimensions (Length, Width, Height)
Default = CMT (Centimeters) 
Also acceptable: MTR (Meters), INH (Inches)

title: and Sample Message
content:
Break Bulk Express (BBX) is a DHL Express service which streamlines destination Customs procedures, 
minimizing Customs lodgment fees charged. It includes the submission of shipment data records for a 
“Mother” shipment and a number of corresponding “Baby” shipments. 
Consolidated BBX is where the Baby shipments are packed together, e.g. on pallets, forming a smaller 
number of pieces of the Mother shipment (e.g. 1 pallet = 1 Mother shipment piece) 
Loose BBX is where the BBX service is used but shipments are not packed together (e.g. not palletized).

title: Appendix E – Commercial Invoice data usage and sample message
content:
Commercial Invoice information must be included in the message for shipments that require declaration 
to Customs, unless such data is sent to DHL Express by an agreed separate data flow. 
If there are multiple invoices for a shipment, each invoice must be given as group in a CNI+9999 segment  
group following the related CNI segment group for that shipment.  The party addresses are given in this  
CNI group.

title: Appendix F – Return Shipments
content:
A “Return” shipment (a shipment of items previously exported from a country, by any carrier, now being 
returned for repair or some other reason) may, depending on the shipment destination country, need to be 
assessed differently by destination Customs. Therefore particular information may need to be provided in 
the manifest data submitted for such a shipment and on any documents also submitted, such as a 
Commercial Invoice. 
 
The Commercial Invoice may be rendered as an image file by DHL Express from the manifest data, which 
can then be made available to the shipper (please contact DHL Express if this solution is required). 
 
The information particular to Return shipments is as follows:
No. 
Information Required 
Location in Message
1 
Original Export Date (of line item within shipment) 
Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item) 
RFF with ZZZ qualifier 
See Page 87 
e.g. RFF+ZZZ:original export date'
Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item) 
RFF with ERN qualifier 
See Page 87 
e.g. RFF+ERN:original export tracking ID'
2 
Original Export Tracking ID (of line item within 
shipment) – i.e. consignment note number of 
shipment on which item/s being returned were 
originally exported
3 
Original Export Carrier Name (of line item within 
shipment) – i.e. carrier of shipment on which item/s 
being returned were originally exported
Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item) 
RFF with CN qualifier 
See Page 87 
e.g. RFF+CN:2 original outbound carrier 
name'
Segment Group 25 (Invoice level) 
FTX with AAU qualifier 
See Page 74 
e.g. FTX+AAU+++Duty free import remarks’
4 
Duty Free Import Remark  – i.e. free text remark to 
assist in Customs submission for Return shipment 
 
Remarks differ by re-import / destination 
country. Examples: 
• 
US: “Goods returning to the US and were not 
advanced in value or condition while outside 
the US”
• 
UK: "British Returned Goods"
• 
CA: "Canadian Goods Return"
• 
AU: “Australian goods returned”
• 
NZ: “New Zealand Returned Goods”
5 
Reason for Export 
Segment Group 25 (Invoice level) 
FTX with CEX qualifier, populate with R 
(Return) 
See page 74 
e.g. FTX+CEX+++R'
Other information mandatory for all dutiable shipments, such as line item descriptions, quantities and 
values, should also be provided
